commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the “(Telegram only)” qualifier from the `/reasoning stream` acknowledgement, updates the thinking docs, and adds mixed-inline directive coverage for the channel-neutral copy.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +47, Docs 0. Total +47 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main returns the stale Telegram-only acknowledgement for `/reasoning stream`, and current Feishu source shows stream-session reasoning previews are supported outside Telegram.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and a sibling docs update are required before merge; automation should not substitute for the contributor’s real environment proof.

target.title: fix: remove telegram-only reasoning stream copy
target.body:
Summary
- Update the `/reasoning stream` acknowledgement to remove the stale Telegram-only qualifier.
- Update `docs/tools/thinking.md` so reasoning stream support is described as channel-support gated instead of Telegram-only.
- Add mixed-inline directive coverage for the channel-neutral acknowledgement.

Replaces stale #72709.

Verification
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.mixed-inline.test.ts`

Behavior addressed: `/reasoning stream` no longer tells users the stream is Telegram-only when other supported channels can stream reasoning.
Real environment tested: local upstream/main-based worktree under `.claude/worktrees/fix-reasoning-stream-ack`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm docs:list`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.mixed-inline.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: the directive test asserts `Reasoning stream enabled.` on a non-Telegram channel surface.
Observed result after fix: Vitest passed with 1 test file and 5 tests.
What was not tested: no live Telegram, Feishu, or Lark channel run was performed.
target.author: lanzhi-lee

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
